author: w3Lb
content: WHY: discovery is parallel (ignore walker) but application is sequential — file_mode/script_mode iterate files one at a time. rg's signature is saturating cores end-to-end. SCOPE: ripsed-cli file_mode + script_mode (json_mode batch can follow the same pattern later). Use rayon par_iter over the discovered file list; each worker does the existing lock->read->apply->backup->write pipeline (per-file FileLock already makes this safe — proven by the concurrent_write_test hammer suite). SHARED STATE to solve: (1) undo log — collect UndoRecords per worker, aggregate + save once at the end (single save_undo_log call, already lock-protected); (2) output ordering — buffer per-file diff output and print in discovery order (rg does the same) so runs stay deterministic; (3) summary counters — AtomicUsize or reduce. --confirm forces sequential mode (prompting from N threads is nonsense). KNOBS: -j/--threads N (default: available_parallelism, like rg). TESTS: result equivalence sequential vs parallel on a fixture tree (same files modified, same bytes, same summary), deterministic output ordering, undo restores everything after a parallel run, --confirm still sequential.
